meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors
family.definition: This major group comprises central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 3. Occupations classified in TEER 3, as those included in this group with a 3 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of a post-secondary education program of less than two years at community college, institute of technology or CÉGEP; or completion of an apprenticeship training program of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training, training courses or specific work experience with some secondary school education; or the accumulation of several years of experience in a related occupation found in TEER 4, when applicable (TEER 4 includes occupations with a 4 as a second digit in their code).
family.major_group_title: Central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Machine operators and supervisors in manufacturing and utilities (92, 94)

units.title: Central control and process operators, mineral and metal processing
units.major_group_title: Central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors
units.sub_major_group_title: Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing
units.minor_group_title: Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing
units.definition: Central control and process operators, mineral and metal processing, operate and monitor multi-function process control machinery and equipment to control the processing of mineral ores, metals or cement. They are employed in mineral ore and metal processing plants such as copper, lead and zinc refineries, uranium processing plants, steel mills, aluminum plants, precious metal refineries and cement processing plants.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Machine operators and supervisors in manufacturing and utilities (92, 94).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Machine operators and supervisors in manufacturing and utilities (92, 94)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Central control and process operators, mineral and metal processing
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Central control and process operators, mineral and metal processing
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Central control and process operators, mineral and metal processing, operate and monitor multi-function process control machinery and equipment to control the processing of mineral ores, metals or cement.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma may be required for some positions in this group.
On-the-job training is provided.
Several years of experience as a machine or process operator, usually in the same company or production department, are required.
additional_information: There is little mobility among the various types of central control and process operators within the mineral and metal processing industry.
With experience, central control and process operators may progress to supervisory positions in mineral and metal processing.

central control room operator - primary metal processing

process operator - primary metal and mineral products processing
refinery process operator - primary metal processing
rolling mill control operator - primary metal processing
slabbing mill roller - primary metal processing
vacuum-degasser process operator - primary metal processing
control room operator - primary metal processing
cupola furnace operator - primary metal processing
electric arc furnace operator - primary metal processing
electric furnace operator - primary metal and mineral products processing
furnace operator - primary metal and mineral products processing
smelting furnace operator - primary metal processing
vacuum furnace operator - primary metal processing
induction furnace operator - mineral and metal processing
open-flame furnace operator - primary metal processing
reverberatory furnace operator - primary metal processing

core_competencies.statement: to maneuver and control automation systems, pumps, valves, conveyors, crushers, or other equipment specific to mineral or metal processing operations

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to detect anomalies, malfunctions, or inefficiencies that may impact mineral or metal processing operations
to optimize the processing of mineral ores or metals through adjusting settings, such as temperature, pressure, flow rates, or chemical dosages
to prevent equipment damage, product quality issues or safety hazards, which require quick response to changes in equipment behaviour
to ensure product quality and process efficiency by conscientiously performing equipment checks and maintaining precise records

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Manufacturing, Processing and Production
core_competencies.statement: to understand the sequence of operations involved in mineral and metal processing which allows to oversee the entire production cycle and identify opportunities for process optimization or improvements

units.title: Central control and process operators, petroleum, gas and chemical processing
units.major_group_title: Central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors
units.sub_major_group_title: Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing
units.minor_group_title: Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing
units.definition: Central control and process operators in petroleum, gas and chemical processing monitor and operate petroleum, petrochemical and chemical plants and monitor, adjust and maintain processing units and equipment in these plants. They are employed by petroleum and natural gas processing, pipeline and petrochemical companies and industrial, agricultural and specialty chemical and pharmaceutical companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Central control and process operators, petroleum, gas and chemical processing
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Central control and process operators, petroleum, gas and chemical processing
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Central control and process operators in petroleum, gas and chemical processing monitor and operate petroleum, petrochemical and chemical plants and monitor, adjust and maintain processing units and equipment in these plants.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required. Mathematics, chemistry and physics subjects are often specified.
A college diploma in process operation, sciences or a related subject may be required for some positions.
Petroleum and chemical process operators and process technicians require completion of several years of formal company training.
Experience as a petroleum or chemical process operator in all the operating units controlled by the central control room is required for chief operators.
Company certification as a petroleum process operator or technician may be required.
A provincial power engineer licence, compressor operator or refrigeration certificate may be required when certain kinds of equipment are included in the process.
Certification in the transportation of dangerous goods (TDG), first aid, firefighting, or workplace hazardous materials information system (WHMIS) may be required.
additional_information: There is considerable mobility among jobs within a company, often as part of a formal development or training program.
Mobility to other petroleum or chemical processing plants is possible, but may be limited by the usual practice of training and promoting workers from within the establishment.
Progression to supervisory or managerial positions is possible with experience.
Personal protective equipment may be required for occupations in this unit group that may involve exposure to hazards of toxic, flammable or explosive chemicals.
workplaces_employers: Industrial, agricultural and specialty chemical companies
Petroleum and natural gas processing companies

example_titles.job_title_text: filter operator - petroleum, gas and chemical processing
gas liquefaction and regasification plant operator
field contract operator - oil and gas

core_competencies.statement: to maneuver and control machinery and equipment in petroleum, petrochemical, or chemical plants, which may include valves, pumps, reactors or distillation columns

core_competencies.competency: Operation Monitoring of Machinery and Equipment
core_competencies.statement: to ensure reliable performance and prevent operational disruptions in petroleum, gas and chemical processing plants
to adjust machine and system settings precisely when operating compressors, scrubbers, evaporators and pumping systems
to choose rapidly between different control actions when lights, sounds or images indicate changes in pressure or temperature
to identify potential hazards, prevent quality deviations and optimize process efficiency by meticulously adjusting and maintaining processing units
to comprehend the chemical reactions and processes involved in petroleum refining, petrochemical production and chemical processing

units.title: Pulping, papermaking and coating control operators
units.major_group_title: Central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors
units.sub_major_group_title: Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing
units.minor_group_title: Central control and process operators in processing and manufacturing
units.definition: Pulping, papermaking and coating control operators operate and monitor multi-function process control machinery and equipment to control the processing of wood, scrap pulp, recyclable paper, cellulose materials, paper pulp and paperboard. They are employed by pulp and paper companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Pulping, papermaking and coating control operators
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Pulping, papermaking and coating control operators
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Pulping, papermaking and coating control operators operate and monitor multi-function process control machinery and equipment to control the processing of wood, scrap pulp, recyclable paper, cellulose materials, paper pulp and paperboard.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college or other program in forest products processing or a related subject may be required for pulping control operators.
Completion of several weeks of formal company training and several months of on-the-job training is required.
Several years of experience as a pulp mill, papermaking or finishing machine operator within the same company is usually required.
A certificate in industrial first aid may be required.
A competency certificate in natural gas may be required.
additional_information: There is limited mobility among jobs within this unit group.
Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: cylinder machine operator - pulp and paper
beater room lead hand - pulp and paper
bleach plant operator - pulp and paper
control room operator - pulp and paper
recovery plant operator - pulp and paper
pulping group operator - pulp and paper
cooking systems operator - pulp and paper
peroxide bleach plant operator - pulp and paper

core_competencies.statement: to identify deviations from set parameters or operational targets through continuously assessing inputs, such as instrumentation readings, data trends and control system outputs
to maneuver and control machinery and equipment, such as pumps, valves, conveyors, drying machines, coating units or other specialized equipment used in paper manufacturing
to detect potential issues by paying attention to sounds indicating normal operation, such as the hum of a machines or the flow of materials through processing lines
to maintain optimal production rates and product quality, notably by adjusting settings, such as feed rates, conveyor speeds and pulp consistency
to address collective challenges affecting the processing operations, such as equipment status, production schedules, or process changes
to understand basic interactions between fibers, chemicals and additives used to modify paper properties

units.title: Aircraft assemblers and aircraft assembly inspectors
units.major_group_title: Central control and process operators and aircraft assembly assemblers and inspectors
units.sub_major_group_title: Aircraft assemblers and aircraft assembly inspectors
units.minor_group_title: Aircraft assemblers and aircraft assembly inspectors
units.definition: Aircraft assemblers assemble, fit and install prefabricated parts to manufacture fixed wing or rotary wing aircraft or aircraft subassemblies. Aircraft assembly inspectors inspect aircraft assemblies for adherence to engineering specifications. They are employed by aircraft and aircraft subassembly manufacturers.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Aircraft assemblers and aircraft assembly inspectors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Aircraft assemblers assemble, fit and install prefabricated parts to manufacture fixed wing or rotary wing aircraft or aircraft subassemblies.
main_duties: Read and interpret aircraft assembly diagrams
Assemble and fit pre-fabricated parts to form subassemblies, working at benches or directly on an aircraft structure
Assemble, fit and install pre-fabricated parts and subassemblies such as aircraft skins, flight controls, rigging, hydraulics and other mechanical systems.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college or other program in aviation or aeronautical technology, with emphasis in aircraft manufacturing, may be required.
Aircraft assemblers receive several months of on-the-job and classroom training.
additional_information: Aircraft assemblers and assembly inspectors may progress to supervisory positions with experience.

example_titles.job_title_text: bench and structural assembler - aircraft assembly

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Aircraft assemblers and aircraft assembly inspectors
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Aircraft assembly inspectors inspect aircraft assemblies for adherence to engineering specifications.
main_duties: Inspect aircraft assemblies, using measuring and testing equipment, for adherence to engineering specifications
Inspect assemblies for alignment, symmetry, dimensions, fit and quality of workmanship
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college or other program in aviation or aeronautical technology, with emphasis in aircraft manufacturing, may be required.
Aircraft assembly inspectors require experience as an aircraft assembler.
additional_information: Aircraft assemblers and assembly inspectors may progress to supervisory positions with experience.
